bad_answer_feedback_path.agent_mcp_durable_path: call borg_record_failure(error_pattern, pack_id, phase, approach, outcome='failure') when the bad path is concrete
bad_answer_feedback_path.human_path: open .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bad-answer.yml or submit redacted first-10 evidence via .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/first-10-evidence.yml

checks.name: stdio_meta_trust_prompt_fails_closed
checks.text_excerpt:
ACTION: proceed with normal debugging for this task; Borg has no proven cache hit.

STOP: do not force a weak or unrelated pack onto this task.

VERIFY: collect the exact failing command/output and rerun borg_observe or borg_rescue only if new evidence appears.

CONFIDENCE: BORG [NO CONFIDENT MATCH] -- no relevant traces, synthetic hits, or pack matches.

NO_CONFIDENT_MATCH: No confident Borg match for this task.
Borg found no relevant real traces, synthetic hits, or exact pack class match.
Proceed with normal reasoning; do not treat Borg as evidence for this task.
After resolving: record concrete bad/good paths with borg_record_failure(...) or borg feedback-v3 when useful.

checks.name: stdio_concrete_permission_prompt_gets_specific_guidance
checks.text_excerpt:
ACTION: from matched seed pack, 1. Check file permissions: ls -la <file>

VERIFY: execute the pack's first checkpoint, then rerun the exact failing command

STOP: do not use chmod 777 or blanket sudo; identify the exact file, owner, and minimum required permission.

CONFIDENCE: Real traces: 0 | Synthetic: 0 | BORG [SYNTHETIC ONLY]

------------------------------------------------------------
WHAT FAILED (7 prior sessions)
   Skip these approaches:
   • 1 agent tried: Ran sudo pip install to fix permission error on system packages
     Why it failed: DEAD END: sudo pip breaks system Python and future pip installs
   • 1 agent tried: Ran sudo npm install -g to fix permission denied error on global package
     Why it failed: DEAD END: sudo npm creates root-owned files in /usr/lib/node_modules breaking all future npm operati
   • 1 agent tried: Added sudo to the failing command in the workflow step
     Why it failed: DEAD END: github-actions runner already runs as appropriate user, sudo causes authentication failure
PACK GUIDANCE (bash-permission-denied)
1. Check file permissions: ls -la <file>
2. Add execute permission: chmod +x <script.sh>
3. For directories: chmod +x <directory>
4

trust_policy: Borg must fail closed with NO_CONFIDENT_MATCH for meta/product/readiness prompts, must not inject unrelated framework guidance, and must preserve concrete error guidance for exact failures.
